questions.question:
Let the characteristic equation of matrix \( M \) be \( \lambda ^{2} - \lambda - 1 = 0 \). Then.



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2010 | Part A | Question: 16
options: \( M^{-1} \) exists but cannot be determined from the data.
\( M^{-1} \) exists and can be determined from the data but the choices (c) and (d) are incorrect.

questions.question:
\( A \) is symmetric positive definite matrix ( i.e., \( x^{T} Ax > 0 \) for all non zero \( x \)). Which of the following statements is false?



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2010 | Part A | Question: 5
options: Sum of the diagonal elements is positive.

questions.question:
Let \( A \) be a matrix such that \( A^{k}=0 \). What is the inverse of \( I - A \)?



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2012 | Part B | Question: 12

How many \( 4 \times 4 \) matrices with entries from \( {0, 1} \) have odd determinant?

Hint: Use modulo \( 2 \) arithmetic.



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2013 | Part B | Question: 3

Consider the following \( 3 \times 3 \) matrices.

\( M_{1}=\begin{pmatrix}   
0&1&1 \\
1&0&1  \\
1&1&0 
\end{pmatrix} \)

\( M_{2}=\begin{pmatrix}   
1&0&1 \\
0&0&0  \\
1&0&1 
\end{pmatrix} \)

How may \( 0-1 \) column vectors of the form

\( X \)= \( \begin{pmatrix}
x_{1} \\
x_{2} \\
x_{3}
\end{pmatrix}       \)

are there such that \( M_{1}X = M_{2}X \) (modulo \( 2 \))? (modulo \( 2 \) means all operations are done modulo \( 2 \), i.e, \( 3 = 1 \) (modulo \( 2 \)), \( 4 = 0 \) (modulo \( 2 \))).



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2015 | Part A | Question: 14

An \( n \times n \) matrix \( M \) with real entries is said to be positive definite if for every non-zero \( n \)-dimensional vector \( x \) with real entries, we have \( x^{T}Mx>0. \) Let  \( A \) and \( B \) be symmetric, positive definite matrices of size \( n\times n \) with real entries.

Consider the following matrices, where \( I \) denotes the \( n\times n \) identity matrix:

  1. \( A+B \)
  2. \( ABA \)
  3. \( A^{2}+I \)

Which of the above matrices must be positive definite?



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2018 | Part A | Question: 12
options: None of the above matrices are positive definite
All of the above matrices are positive definite

questions.question:
Let \( A \) be an \( n\times n \) invertible matrix with real entries whose row sums are all equal to \( c \). Consider the following statements:

  1. Every row in the matrix \( 2A \) sums to \( 2c \).
  2. Every row in the matrix \( A^{2} \) sums to \( c^{2} \).
  3. Every row in the matrix \( A^{-1} \) sums to \( c^{-1} \).

Which of the following is TRUE?



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2018 | Part A | Question: 14
options: statement \( (1) \) is correct but not necessarily statements \( (2) \) or \( (3) \)
statement \( (2) \) is correct but not necessarily statements \( (1) \) or \( (3) \)
statement \( (1) \)  and \( (2) \) are correct but not necessarily statement \( (3) \)
all the three statements \( (1), (2), \) and \( (3) \) are correct

questions.question:
Let \( A \) be am \( n\times n \) invertible matrix with real entries whose column sums are all equal to \( 1 \). Consider the following statements:

  1. Every column in the matrix \( A^{2} \) sums to \( 2 \)
  2. Every column in the matrix \( A^{3} \) sums to \( 3 \)
  3. Every column in the matrix \( A^{-1} \) sums to \( 1 \)

Which of the following is TRUE?



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2020 | Part A | Question: 5
options: statement \( (1) \) is correct but not statements \( (2) \) or \( (3) \)
statement \( (2) \) is correct but not statements \( (1) \) or \( (3) \)
statement \( (3) \) is correct but not statements \( (1) \) or \( (2) \)
questions.correct_answer: statement \( (3) \) is correct but not statements \( (1) \) or \( (2) \)

questions.question:
Let \( A \) and \( B \) be two matrices of size \( n \times n \) and with real-valued entries. Consider the following statements.

  1. If \( AB = B \), then \( A \) must be the identity matrix.
  2. If \( A \) is an idempotent (i.e. \( A^{2} = A \)) nonsingular matrix, then \( A \) must be the identity matrix.
  3. If \( A^{-1} = A \), then \( A \) must be the identity matrix.

Which of the above statements \( \text{MUST} \) be true of \( A \)?



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2021 | Part B | Question: 8

Consider the \( n \times n \) matrix \( M \) defined as follows:
\[ M=\left(\begin{array}{cccc}
1 & 2 & \ldots & n \\
n+1 & n+2 & \ldots & 2 n \\
2 n+1 & 2 n+2 & \ldots & 3 n \\
\vdots & \vdots & \ldots & \vdots \\
(n-1) n+1 & (n-1) n+2 & \ldots & n^{2}
\end{array}\right). \]
Let \( M_{i, j} \) denote the entry present in the \( i \)-th row and the \( j \)-th column of \( M \) for each \( 1 \leq i, j \leq n \). Define a set \( S \) as
\[
S=\left\{M_{i_{1}, j_{1}}+M_{i_{2}, j_{2}}+\cdots+M_{i_{n}, j_{n}} \mid\left(i_{1}, \ldots, i_{n}\right) \text { and }\left(j_{1}, \ldots, j_{n}\right)\right.
\]
are permutations of \( [n]\} \).

(Here, \( [n] \) denotes the set \( \{1,2, \ldots, n\} \) of positive integers from \( 1 \) to \( n.) \) How many elements does \( S \) have?



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2023 | Part A | Question: 15

Suppose \( A \) is a \( 2 \times 2 \) matrix such that the sum of the principal diagonal entries of \( A \) is \( 10 \) and the sum of the principal diagonal entries of \( A^{2} \) is \( 20 \). (For any \( 2 \times 2 \) matrix \( B \), the principal diagonal entries of \( B \) are the entries \( B_{1,1} \) and \( B_{2,2}.) \) What is \( \operatorname{det}(A)? \) 



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2023 | Part A | Question: 5
options: Nonzero, but cannot be uniquely determined from the above data.
Cannot be uniquely determined from the above data, and could also be zero.

questions.question:
Let \( \text{M} \) be a \( 3 \times 3 \) matrix over the real numbers such that \( \text{M}^{\text{T}} \text{M}=\mathbf{I} \). Consider the following statements.

  1. There exists a non-zero vector \( x \in \mathbb{R}^{3} \) such that \( M x=\mathbf{0} \).
  2. There exist two non-zero vectors \( x, y \in \mathbb{R}^{3} \) such that the angle between them and that between \( \text{M x} \) and \( \text{M y} \) are different.
  3. There exists a non-zero vector \( \text{x} \) such that its length is different from that of \( \text{M x} \).

Which of the above statements is/are true?



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2024 | Part A | Question: 10

Let \( \text{A} \) and \( \text{B} \) be two \( n \times n \) invertible matrices with real entries such that every row in \( \text{A} \) sums to \( 1 \) and every row in \( \text{B} \) sums to \( 2 \). Consider the following three statements:

  1. Every row in the matrix \( \text{AB} \) sums to \( 2 \).
  2. Every row in the matrix \( \text{A}^{-1} B \) sums to \( 2 \).
  3. Every row in the matrix \( \text{A}^{-1} \text{B}^{-1} \) sums to \( \frac{1}{2} \).

Which of the above statements is/are true?



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2024 | Part A | Question: 14
options: All the three statements \( \text{(i), (ii)} \), and \( \text{(iii)} \) are true.
Statement \( \text{(i)} \) is true but not necessarily statements \( \text{(ii)} \) or \( \text{(iii)} \).
Statements \( \text{(i)} \) and \( \text{(ii)} \) are true but not necessarily statement \( \text{(iii)} \).
Statements \( \text{(i)} \) and \( \text{(iii)} \) are true but not necessarily statement \( \text{(ii)} \).
questions.correct_answer: All the three statements \( \text{(i), (ii)} \), and \( \text{(iii)} \) are true.
